name: Rafa's Cafe Mexicaon Homestyle Salsa
recipeIngredient: 3 cans tomatoes, canned, water packed with juices
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a heavy skillet (cast iron is good) over high heat until very hot.
2. Cook the chilies in a dry pan on all sides until the skins blister and char.
3. Place the roasted chilies in a plastic bag and seal it. Let "sweat" for 3 to 4 minutes.
- Longer sweating will overcook the chiles.
4. Remove the chilies from the bag and rinse under cold running water.
- Be careful not to touch your eyes, lips, or nose while handling the chilies.
- Wash hands thoroughly to remove any fiery juices.
5. Discard the skins. Make a small slit on one side of each chili and remove the seeds, veins, and stems.
6. Place the prepared chilies and 1 can of tomatoes in a blender. Pulse a few times, just enough to break up the tomatoes without pureeing them.
7. Pour the contents into a large bowl.
8. Add the remaining tomatoes (1 can at a time) to the bowl, along with salt, granulated garlic, black pepper, olive oil, and chopped cilantro.
9. Stir until well mixed.
10. Makes about 3 quarts.
11. Refrigerate any unused salsa; it improves if made a day in advance.
